name: video-editor-final-polish-20260325-0602
idea: Video editor final polish round: (1) When timeline is locked/expanded on editor page, shrink video preview to give timeline more space, revert when unlocked; (2) Fix render - it worked in announcement-video, figure out what's missing; (3) Timeline clip dragging has no visual feedback showing where clip is being dragged to; (4) Text overlay dragging is sticky - container moves but text doesn't follow; (5) Image overlay dragging is sticky/inconsistent; (6) Condense timeline controls - move clear-unused, new-track buttons, zoom into the empty row space; (7) Clear X unused shouldn't count default V1/A1 tracks; (8) EditorPane should overlay content like GenerationsPane, not push it down; (9) Playhead in compact EditorPane not clickable

overrides.note:
CRITICAL FORMAT: Use FLAT ## Step N: headings at h2 level. NO ## Phase headers.

DETAILED CONTEXT FOR EACH BUG:

1. PREVIEW RESIZE WHEN TIMELINE LOCKED:
- VideoEditorShell.tsx has the CSS grid layout with preview (top) and timeline (bottom)
- When user locks/expands the timeline panel (by dragging the divider or clicking), the preview should shrink to a minimum (e.g. 150-200px) to give timeline maximum space
- When unlocked/collapsed, revert to the original split
- This is about the resizable divider between preview and timeline in the full editor mode

2. RENDER NOT WORKING:
- The announcement-video app has a working render system at viewer/vite.config.ts that spawns python3 tools/render.py
- That approach uses Remotion CLI to render server-side
- In reigh-app we don't have the Python render tooling, but we CAN use @remotion/renderer or the Remotion Lambda approach
- For now, the simplest approach: use the Remotion Player's built-in export/download functionality
- Or: implement a client-side render using canvas capture (MediaRecorder API)
- Check what the announcement-video render button actually does and replicate the UX

3. TIMELINE CLIP DRAG - NO VISUAL FEEDBACK:
- @xzdarcy/react-timeline-editor handles clip dragging
- The issue is that during drag, there's no visual ghost/preview showing where the clip will land
- This could be a CSS issue - the drag ghost might be invisible or positioned wrong
- Check the timeline-overrides.css and the ClipAction component
- The library may need custom drag rendering via getActionRender callback

4. TEXT OVERLAY DRAGGING STICKY:
- OverlayEditor.tsx handles drag for clips in the preview canvas
- When dragging a text clip, the container/bounding box moves but the text content doesn't follow
- This is likely because the text rendering is done by Remotion (which shows the PREVIOUS frame position) while the overlay box shows the NEW position
- The fix: during drag, update the clip's x/y position in real-time so Remotion re-renders the text at the new position
- Could also be that the drag handler only updates position on drag END, not during drag

5. IMAGE OVERLAY DRAGGING STICKY:
- Same issue as text but for image/video clips
- During drag, the clip position should update continuously, not just on drop
- Check if onDrag/onMouseMove handler calls position update or only onMouseUp
- Could be related to the save/dirty cycle interrupting the drag

6. CONDENSE TIMELINE CONTROLS:
- Currently there's toolbar with: track type filter icons, "clear X unused" link, zoom in/out buttons
- And there's an empty row of space below the track labels
- Move all these controls into a more compact layout, possibly inline with the track area
- The goal is to reduce vertical space waste

7. CLEAR X UNUSED - DON'T COUNT DEFAULTS:
- The "clear 2 unused" link counts V1 and A1 as unused when they have no clips
- But V1 and A1 are default tracks that should always be present
- Fix: exclude tracks that are in DEFAULT_VIDEO_TRACKS from the unused count

8. EDITOR PANE OVERLAY vs PUSH:
- EditorPaneSurface slides down from top and currently PUSHES main content down (LayoutMainContent adds padding-top)
- It should OVERLAY content like GenerationsPane does (fixed position, content behind it)
- Look at how GenerationsPane works - it's fixed position and doesn't push content
- The EditorPane should behave the same way - overlay, not push
- Check LayoutMainContent.tsx for where editorPaneHeight adds padding

9. PLAYHEAD NOT CLICKABLE IN COMPACT:
- CompactPreview has a slider/scrub bar for the playhead
- The playhead can't be clicked/dragged
- This could be a z-index issue or the slider component not receiving events
- Check if there's an overlay blocking click events on the slider

ADDITIONAL BUGS FROM USER:

10. EXTERNAL IMAGE DRAG-DROP BROKEN:
- Dragging external images (from desktop/finder) onto the timeline shows hover state but doesn't actually upload
- It should upload to Supabase Storage the same way generations do
- The uploaded file should be saved as a generation first, then added to the timeline
- Use the existing skeleton/placeholder functionality during upload
- Check the file drop handler in TimelineEditor or AssetPanel for the upload flow

11. ASSETS PANEL DEFAULT CLOSED:
- Assets panel in PropertiesPanel should be collapsed/closed by default
- May already be implemented from previous megaplan, but verify

MORE BUGS FROM CONSOLE:

12. COMPACT PREVIEW SLIDER CRASH: CompactPreview.tsx:80 has onValueChange that does destructuring [value] but the slider passes a single number not an array. Fix: change onValueChange={([value]) => ...} to onValueChange={(value) => ...}. This is the PLAYHEAD BUG - it crashes because the slider's API changed.

13. REMOTION LICENSE WARNING: Pass acknowledgeRemotionLicense prop to <Player /> to suppress the warning.

14. DUPLICATE QUERIES WARNING: [QueriesObserver]: Duplicate Queries found. This likely comes from having both the compact EditorPane and the full editor page mounting the same timeline query simultaneously, or from the useTimelineData polling. Investigate and deduplicate.

USER SAYS: Plan thoroughly and execute it all! Go go go!

REVISION GUIDANCE:

1. duplicate-queries-already-guarded: The EditorPane returns null on the editor route, so duplicates aren't from coexisting providers. More likely: React strict mode double-mount, or useTimelineData + useTimelinesList both mounting with similar query keys. Fix by adding unique query key prefixes and checking for strict mode double-mount.

2. overlay-drag-perf-risk: Don't update the full pipeline on every mouse move. Instead, use local state for the drag offset (just move the overlay div visually) and only commit the final position on mouseUp. The Remotion preview doesn't need to update during drag - just show the bounding box moving.

3. render-canvas-capture-speculative: Remotion Player renders DOM, not canvas. For client-side render, we can't use captureStream. Options: (a) just disable render for now with a clear "Render not available yet" message, (b) use html2canvas to capture frames (slow but works), (c) punt to server-side. Go with option (a) for now - show toast, keep infrastructure for when server-side is ready.

4. editor-pane-overlay: The user wants it to behave like the generations pane at the bottom. If generations pane pushes when locked, then editor pane should also push when locked. If generations pane overlays when just hovering, editor pane should too. Just match the behavior exactly.

5. assets-panel-already-closed: Remove this step since it's already done.

6. condense-controls: Just commit to the simplest approach - put the controls inline in the toolbar row and remove the empty space below.

last_gate.rationale: All three significant flags have been directly addressed by the user's revision guidance. (1) duplicate-queries: user clarified it's not coexisting providers, investigate strict mode / shared query keys instead. (2) overlay-drag-perf: user specified local-state-only approach during drag, commit on mouseUp only — no full pipeline on every move. (3) render: user chose option (a) — disable with clear message, keep infrastructure for later. The revision guidance is actionable and resolves each flag. No plan revision needed; the executor should follow revision guidance where it overrides original plan text.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Iteration 1, weighted score 5.0 with 3 significant flags — all addressed by user revision guidance before gate. No recurring critiques, no scope creep, no plan delta history. Preflight clean. The flags were real concerns but the user preemptively provided concrete fixes for each, making them settled decisions rather than open risks.
warnings: Step 5/6: Executor must use local drag offset state only — do NOT call onOverlayChange during mousemove as the original plan suggests. Follow revision guidance.
Step 9: Do NOT attempt canvas capture. Show 'Render not available yet' toast and preserve existing infrastructure.
Step 13: Do NOT assume coexisting providers. Investigate React strict mode double-mount and shared query keys as root causes.
Step 11 (assets panel default closed): User says already done — verify and skip if confirmed.

settled_decisions.decision: Render button shows 'Render not available yet' toast. No client-side capture attempt. Keep existing startRender infrastructure for future server-side implementation.
settled_decisions.rationale: User explicitly chose option (a). Remotion Player renders DOM not canvas, so captureStream is not viable. Server-side render is the correct long-term path.

settled_decisions.decision: Overlay drag (text/image) uses local state offset to move the bounding box visually. Only commit final position via onOverlayChange on mouseUp. Remotion preview does NOT re-render during drag.
settled_decisions.rationale: User specified this approach to avoid the full materializeData pipeline on every mousemove. The visual bounding box movement is sufficient feedback.

settled_decisions.decision: Skip assets panel default-closed step if already implemented. Verify only.
settled_decisions.rationale: User confirmed this may already be done from a previous megaplan.

settled_decisions.decision: EditorPane overlays content when open but not locked. Pushes content only when locked, matching GenerationsPane behavior.
settled_decisions.rationale: Plan option (b) from Step 4, consistent with how the bottom GenerationsPane works.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: First iteration; follow gate recommendation: PROCEED. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
